title: Section
description: A section heading from a rrxiv paper. The section hierarchy is the document structure used by clients that browse/navigate papers and by claim queries that filter by section.

id.description: Stable section ID within the paper. Usually the LaTeX label (e.g. 'sec:intro').

type.description: Section level. Maps from LaTeX \section / \subsection / \subsubsection / \paragraph and the abstract environment.

order.description: Canonical display order within the paper. Sections of the same level use this as the tiebreaker.

parent_id.description: ID of the parent section if this is nested. Null for top-level sections.

raw_source.description: Raw source text of this section, captured if the parser was instructed to preserve it.

rendered_html.description: Rendered HTML form of this section, if a renderer produced one.

claims_in_section.description: Reverse index from section to claims that appear in it.
